skill.name: data-quality-frameworks
skill.description: Implement data quality validation with Great Expectations, dbt tests, and data contracts. Use when building data quality pipelines, implementing validation rules, or establishing data contracts.
skill.summary: Production patterns for implementing data quality with Great Expectations, dbt tests, and data contracts to ensure reliable data pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing markdown guides for data quality frameworks. All static analysis findings are false positives: code blocks are markdown examples not executable code, URLs are documentation references, and pattern matches on SQL terms are not actual system calls.

content.user_title: Build Data Quality Pipelines with Validation Frameworks
content.value_statement: Ensure reliable data pipelines with comprehensive validation using Great Expectations, dbt tests, and data contracts. Reduce data incidents and build trust in your analytics with production-ready quality patterns.
actual_capabilities: Generate Great Expectations suites with schema, uniqueness, and validity expectations
Create dbt test configurations for column-level and relationship validation
Design data contract specifications with quality SLAs and schema definitions
Build automated data quality pipelines with checkpoint reporting
Implement custom dbt tests for business-specific validation rules
Configure alerting and notification workflows for quality failures
limitations: Does not execute validation code - provides configuration patterns only
Requires user to have data platform access (Snowflake, BigQuery, etc.)
Framework-specific syntax may need adaptation for your environment
Cannot validate data without connection to actual data sources

use_cases.title: Analytics Engineer Building dbt Tests
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive test suites for your dbt models with column-level validations, relationship checks, and custom business rules.
use_cases.target_user: Analytics engineers using dbt for data transformation

use_cases.title: Data Platform Team Establishing Contracts
use_cases.description: Define data contracts between data producers and consumers with clear schema expectations, quality SLAs, and ownership.
use_cases.target_user: Data platform teams managing cross-team data dependencies

use_cases.title: Data Quality Lead Implementing Great Expectations
use_cases.description: Deploy enterprise-grade data quality validation with expectation suites, checkpoints, and automated reporting dashboards.
use_cases.target_user: Data quality engineers building validation pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic Column Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Great Expectations suite for an orders table with order_id as primary key. Include expectations for not null, unique, and valid order status values (pending, processing, shipped, delivered, cancelled).
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start with basic column-level validation

prompt_templates.title: dbt Test Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate dbt schema.yml test configurations for a customer dimension table. Include unique and not_null tests for customer_id, accepted_values for status, and a relationship test to verify referential integrity.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up dbt tests for dimension tables

prompt_templates.title: Data Contract Specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a data contract for order events streaming from an e-commerce platform. Include schema fields with types, PII classifications, quality checks using SodaCL syntax, and SLA definitions for freshness and availability.
prompt_templates.scenario: Establishing contracts between data producers and consumers

prompt_templates.title: Custom Business Rule Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a custom dbt test that validates order totals are consistent: subtotal + tax + shipping must equal total_amount within a 0.01 tolerance. Include the full macro and usage example.

output_examples.input: Generate Great Expectations checkpoint for daily orders validation with Slack alerts
output: Checkpoint configured with daily validation schedule
Actions: store results, update Data Docs, send Slack on failure
Webhook integration using SLACK_WEBHOOK environment variable

output_examples.input: Create data contract for user events with PII handling
output: email field marked as PII with indirect classification
SLA: 99.9% availability, 1 hour freshness, 5 minute latency

best_practices: Test early in the pipeline - validate source data before transformations to catch issues at ingestion
Focus on critical columns - prioritize high-impact fields over exhaustive coverage
Document each expectation with clear descriptions so team members understand the business rule
anti_patterns: Blocking production pipelines without fallback - always have a manual override path for critical data flows
Testing in isolation - validate relationships between tables, not just individual column constraints
Hardcoding thresholds - use dynamic baselines and statistical ranges that adapt to data growth

faq.question: What is the difference between Great Expectations and dbt tests?
faq.answer: Great Expectations provides rich expectation suites with detailed validation reports and Data Docs. dbt tests are SQL-based and run as part of the dbt build process. Use both: dbt for transformation-time checks, Great Expectations for deeper validation.

faq.question: How do I handle PII data in validation outputs?
faq.answer: Never include raw PII values in validation results or reports. Use aggregation, masking, or only report row counts and statistical summaries. Mark PII fields in data contracts and apply extra access controls.

faq.question: What should I do when a data quality check fails?
faq.answer: First, verify if it is a data issue or a test issue. Alert the data owner, quarantine affected data if blocking, and document the incident. Use failures to improve tests and prevent recurrence.

faq.question: How do I version data contracts?
faq.answer: Treat contracts as code: store in git, use semantic versioning, and maintain a changelog. For breaking changes, increment the major version and provide migration guidance to consumers.

faq.question: Can I run Great Expectations in my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Yes. Run lightweight expectation suites in CI to catch schema changes and critical violations. Schedule comprehensive suites separately to avoid slowing down deployments.

faq.question: What metrics should I track for data quality?
faq.answer: Track pass rate by suite, mean time to detect failures, mean time to resolution, and trend analysis over time. Monitor freshness, completeness, and validity dimensions separately.
